title: User receives notification on post interactions
description: The system shall notify a user via email, push, or in-app when someone likes or comments on their published post.
rationale: To keep users informed about interactions with their content, ensuring they stay engaged and connected with their audience.
business_value: Increased engagement from followers by promptly receiving notifications of likes and comments, potentially leading to more interaction and community building.

source.description: As a user, I need to receive notifications when someone likes or comments on my post so that I can stay updated on interactions with my post.

acceptance_criteria: Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When another user likes my post, Then I receive a notification that someone liked my post.
Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When another user comments on my post, Then I receive a notification that someone commented on my post.
Given I am a logged-in user with a published post, When I have disabled notifications for that post, And another user likes or comments on my post, Then I do not receive any notification.
preconditions: The user must be logged in to the system
The user's account must have notifications enabled for post interactions
The post must be published and accessible to other users
postconditions: The user receives a notification if their post is liked or commented on by another user
No notification is received if the user has disabled notifications for that specific post
functional_constraints: Notifications may be delivered via email, push, or in-app
The system must track likes and comments on posts
The system must differentiate between types of notifications (likes vs. comments)
security_requirements: User authentication is required to receive notifications
Notifications should not contain sensitive user information

non_functional_requirements.performance: Notification delivery within a maximum time frame
non_functional_requirements.availability: High availability of the notification system
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle an increasing number of users and posts without performance degradation
non_functional_requirements.usability: Notifications should be easily visible and understandable to the user

edge_cases: What happens if a user has multiple devices and notifications are enabled on all of them?
How does the system handle high traffic periods when many users interact with posts?
What if a user changes their notification preferences while an interaction is being processed?

interfaces.name: Notification Service API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to send notifications via email, push, or in-app.

interfaces.name: User Preferences Database
interfaces.description: The requirement reads from and writes to this database to determine if notifications are enabled for specific posts.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user receives a notification when another user likes their post.
test_cases.expected_result: The system sends a notification to the user's preferred method (email, push, or in-app) confirming that someone liked their post.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user receives a notification when another user comments on their post.
test_cases.expected_result: The system sends a notification to the user's preferred method (email, push, or in-app) confirming that someone commented on their post.

test_cases.description: A logged-in user does not receive a notification when another user likes or comments on their post if notifications are disabled.
test_cases.expected_result: The system does not send any notification to the user, and no record of attempted notification is created.
